title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard from Google Chrome that replaces DOM parsing and screenshot-based navigation with structured, named tools that agents call directly. It offers both declarative HTML attribute APIs and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering custom agent-callable actions, currently in early preview on Chrome 146.
writeup: The talk introduces WebMCP, a web standard proposed by Google Chrome that lets sites expose structured, named tools agents call directly, replacing DOM parsing and screenshot-based navigation. It matters because it shifts agent interaction from brittle visual inference to explicit, registered actions defined by the site. Key takeaways: WebMCP offers a declarative HTML attribute API and an imperative JavaScript API for registering custom agent-callable actions, and it is in early preview on Chrome 146.
